title: The Rat.
content:
The rat is the concisest tenant.
    He pays no rent, --
    Repudiates the obligation,
    On schemes intent.

    Balking our wit
    To sound or circumvent,
    Hate cannot harm
    A foe so reticent.

    Neither decree
    Prohibits him,
    Lawful as
    Equilibrium.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "The Rat.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
